objective: Optimize CleanMac scrolling performance and remove the main sources of visible jank.
exit_condition: The goal is complete when scrolling-related SwiftUI hotspots have been inspected, the highest-impact jank source is fixed without weakening product behavior, focused source/test coverage or existing tests validate the change, the app builds/tests successfully, ./script/build_and_run.sh --verify confirms the app launches, .loopgoal/state.json records the final state, and the intended changes are committed.

phases.name: Initialize checkpoint and establish current repo state

current.focus: Baseline verify_cmd passed after sandbox escalation. Read-only investigation identified reusable illustration timelines, repeated bundle image loading, eager scroll-page layout, animated page background, and repeated candidate aggregation as the main scrolling jank risks.
current.next_action: Add focused source guards, then optimize static illustration rendering and scroll-page layout.

decisions.reason: The user requested a result-driven performance optimization, not a recurring time-based loop.

decisions.decision: Keep the exit condition tied to build/test plus launch verification rather than subjective smoothness alone.
decisions.reason: The report is broad and local performance instrumentation may be limited, so the checkpoint needs a concrete completion gate while still targeting the visible scrolling jank.

decisions.decision: Prioritize the reusable illustration and page-layout path before deeper data-model refactors.
decisions.reason: Both independent read-only investigations and main-agent review found CleanMacFeatureImage and CleanMacPulseIcon are used throughout scrolling surfaces while always hosting TimelineView(.animation), and CleanMacFeatureImage reloads bundle PNGs from body-time code.

decisions.decision: Keep subagents read-only for this goal.
decisions.reason: The user-provided AGENTS.md instructions forbid subagents from editing files, so the main agent owns checkpoint writes, implementation, validation, and commits.
